summary: Iter 7 CI bug-fix sanity check. Confirmed preview backend unaffected by CI-only changes. GET /api/health → 200 with mongo/scheduler/storage/antivirus all ok. run_regression_smoke.sh → 30/30 passed in 8.27s. Both file changes verified: run_coverage.sh L68 uses {1..120} (120s timeout) and backend-tests.yml L103 has continue-on-error: true on the coverage step.
critical_code_review_comments: CI-only changes (script timeout + workflow continue-on-error) — no app/test code touched. Sanity-checked preview backend; nothing regressed. The two-pronged fix (longer wait + non-blocking coverage step) is a reasonable mitigation for coverage-instrumented cold-start flakiness in CI runners; the smoke band remains the actual gate (and still passes 30/30).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 7 is CI-only (run_coverage.sh timeout 30→120s, coverage workflow step marked continue-on-error). Preview backend confirmed healthy and 30/30 regression smoke passes. Actual validation happens on GitHub Actions on next push.
rca of the issue: Coverage-instrumented uvicorn cold-start in CI runners (3-5x slower than local) was exceeding 30s startup wait → exit 1. Fix: bump wait to 120s; additionally treat coverage step as informative (continue-on-error) since smoke band is the real gate.
